However, season NetRtg: NYK +6.6 vs ATL +2.6 — gap of 4.0 points in NYK's favour. L15 gap favours ATL directionally but the season NetRtg strongly favours NYK (+6.6 vs +2.6, gap of 4.0 points = meaningful edge signal). ATL's L15 surge is noted but the hot streak fade rule applies. | HOT STREAK FADE: ATL Hawks L10: 8-2, W4 streak, season .577 W% — overall record implies ~.550 true talent. NYK at 2.02 qualifies as ≥ 1.80, so +10 confidence for regression-fade. ATL NetRtg +2.6 is genuinely below their W% implied talent — regression signal real. | STEP 2 — B2B/Schedule: No B2B issues noted for either team. | STEP 3 — Franchise players: NYK injury report shows only Trey Jemison III Out (G League) — no franchise player concerns. ATL: Jock Landale Out (confirmed), RayJ Dennis Questionable, Keshon Gilbert Questionable (both G League). No franchise player absences for either team. | STEP 4 — Play-in/Seeding: NYK 50-28 fighting for top seed in East — high motivation. ATL 45-33 in play-in race — also motivated. | STEP 5 — L10: NYK 7-3 vs ATL 8-2 — ATL slight edge in L10. | STEP 6 — Home court: ATL home, worth ~2-3 pts (~0.10-0.15 odds). NetRtg gap < 3 points between teams (4.0 gap slightly above threshold but home court relevant). Books pricing ATL -1.5 home favourite despite NYK's better record and NetRtg. | STEP 7 — DefRtg: NYK 113.4 vs ATL 113.5 — essentially identical. No significant spread edge from defense. | STEP 9 — Pace: Pace data shows ATL 101.7 vs NYK 97.1 — mismatch of 4.6 points (just below 5-point mismatch threshold). Given this is an away team pick against a hot home favourite, use lower end of High tier = €150 (15% — cautious due to ATL's impressive L15 surge).

scout_report_data.report: Tonight's 5-game slate yields 3 draft picks across ML and spread markets. Injury feed source is NBA Official (full coverage) — normal confidence rules apply. Pace data is available this session (Pace values populated). Key themes: (1) NYK ML vs ATL exploits the hot-streak fade rule — ATL's L10 8-2 surge diverges from their +2.6 season NetRtg, and NYK's superior +6.6 NetRtg makes 2.02 valuable; (2) SAS -8.5 leverages Spurs' elite L15 (+48.9) against PHI's confirmed negative NetRtg (-0.2) fade candidate on the road; (3) DEN -8.5 backed by Portland's triple key absence (Lillard OUT, Grant OUT, Sharpe OUT) neutralising POR's surprising L15 figure. CLE vs MEM rejected due to ML odds out of range and dual Allen/Mobley absence uncertainty. DET vs ORL rejected due to Cunningham confirmed out per NBA official PDF (franchise player rule) and ORL's deeply negative L15 (-25.7). Total exposure: €400 of €1000 bankroll (40% — within 70% max).
